subject: [PATCH v6 07/10] dax: plumb hotplug online_type through dax

2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch adds the ability for drivers using dax_kmem to specify an auto-online policy when creating a DAX device. It introduces a new field, 'online_type', which can be set to override the system default online policy. The system default is preserved and can still be used by setting it explicitly. This change allows for more flexibility in handling hotplug events and ensures that the ABI remains consistent even if the system default changes over time.

reviews.author: Dan (nvidia)
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Dan (nvidia) noted that the current patch does not utilize the newly added online-type field, and questioned whether it is necessary to plumb this through to __add_memory_driver_managed()

Reviewer questioned whether DAX_ONLINE_DEFAULT should be used as a sentinel for 'default' policy, suggesting it might not be clear to users how to set the policy, and asked if DAX_KMEM_UNPLUGGED should be a different sentinel than (-1).
reviews.raw_body:
So I know you have some future usage for this ability, but it is not
present in this set. The only piece that *is* used is that the
online-type from the new sysfs interface gets plumbed through to
__add_memory_driver_managed().

Are these touches:

---

...premature until the first user arrives that with the background story
about how it knows to set the policy?

If DAX_ONLINE_DEFAULT is a sentinel for "default" should
DAX_KMEM_UNPLUGGED be a different sentinel than (-1)?

Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Dan Williams <djbw@kernel.org>

...to this and the previous patches when that is fixed up.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged that they didn't want to cross multiple subsystems in one patch, confirmed plans to add CXL build option to override global hotplug policy.

Author acknowledged a potential issue with default values being set inconsistently across dax driver variant probe functions, and is willing to remove the DEFAULT setting if deemed unnecessary.

Author acknowledged that DAX_ONLINE_DEFAULT could be perceived as a system default policy by 'dax/state', agreed to change its value to -2 to avoid visual confusion.
reviews.raw_body:
Correct.

I didn't want to cross three subsystems in one go, I do intend to follow
this up with at least a CXL build option to override the global hotplug
policy by plumbing it through to the existing cxl auto-probe process.

Some of the accelerator stuff is still a bit up in the air but the base
driver can still benefit from this as well.

---

This was more a matter of having the DEFAULT set consistently across
the dax driver variant probe() functions to make the behavior explicit.
I didn't want an un-set value bug to creep in here somehow.

Happy to drop them if you think that's unneeded.

---

They don't actually run into each other.  DAX_ONLINE_DEFAULT is
overwritten at probe time with the system default policy, so
`dax/state` can never perceive it (even if the values are the same).

But this is visually confusing i suppose, so I'll just swap it for -2.

reviews.author: Gregory Price (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that not setting the value in each place is equivalent to setting MMOP_OFFLINE (0), decided to keep the current implementation, and implied no further revision will be made.
reviews.raw_body:
Ah

Not setting the value in each of those places is equivalent to setting
MMOP_OFFLINE (0), so better to just set DEFAULT regardless.

So unless you have strong feelings i will keep them as-is.

~Gregory

2026-07-09.patch_summary: This patch adds the ability for drivers using dax_kmem to specify an auto-online policy when creating a DAX device. It introduces a new field, 'online_type', which allows drivers to override the system default policy. The system default policy is now resolved at kmem driver bind time, rather than at device creation time, to avoid freezing stale policies. This change does not affect existing callers, as they still use the system default policy.

reviews.author: Dan (nvidia)
reviews.summary: Reviewer Dan noted that the changelog incorrectly states that device-dax drivers cannot specify their online type besides the default, when in fact this series only maintains the status quo by allowing unconditional use of mhp_get_default_online_type() in dev_dax_kmem_probe().
sentiment_signals: mismatch between changelog and actual code changes
reviews.raw_body:
Right, the mild feelings are only coming from the changelog mismatch
which says "Oh no, device-dax drivers can not specify their online type
besides the default" and all this series does is keep the status quo.

That can be had by just having unconditional:

     online_type = mhp_get_default_online_type();

...in dev_dax_kmem_probe() and get rid of dev_dax->online_type until the
first user arrives. If you are respinning the series and that patch
drops, yay. If not, oh well.

2026-07-10.patch_summary: This patch adds the ability for drivers using dax_kmem to specify an auto-online policy when creating a DAX device. It introduces a new field, 'online_type', which allows drivers to override the system default policy. The system default policy is now resolved at kmem driver bind time, rather than at device creation time, to avoid freezing stale policies. This change does not affect existing callers, as they still use the system default policy.
